3d:["$","$L141",null,{"children":["$","code",null,{"className":"hljs language-text","children":"# REINFORCE: Monte Carlo Policy Gradient\n# Input: differentiable policy π_θ, learning rate α\nfor episode = 1..M:\n    generate trajectory τ = (s₀, a₀, r₁, ..., s_T) using π_θ\n    for t = 0..T-1:\n        G_t = Σ_{k=0}^{T-t-1} γᵏ r_{t+k+1}           # monte carlo return\n        θ ← θ + α γᵗ ∇_θ log π_θ(a_t | s_t) G_t       # policy gradient update\n"}]}]

6a:["$","p",null,{"style":{"fontSize":"1rem","color":"var(--text-body)","lineHeight":1.75,"marginBottom":"1.25em","marginTop":0},"children":[["$","strong",null,{"style":{"color":"var(--text-strong)","fontWeight":600},"children":["Advantage Actor-Critic (",["$","$L10",null,{"term":"A2C"}],")"]}]," applies the actor-critic update synchronously: all\nparallel workers complete their rollouts, gradients are aggregated, and a single\nsynchronized update is applied. 6b:["$","p",null,{"style":{"fontSize":"1rem","color":"var(--text-body)","lineHeight":1.75,"marginBottom":"1.25em","marginTop":0},"children":[["$","strong",null,{"style":{"color":"var(--text-strong)","fontWeight":600},"children":["Asynchronous Advantage Actor-Critic (",["$","$L10",null,{"term":"A3C"}],")"]}]," runs workers asynchronously — each\nworker pushes gradient updates to a shared parameter server without waiting for others.\nThe asynchrony decorrelates experience across workers (similar to a replay buffer) but\nwithout requiring off-policy corrections, since each worker runs the current policy.\n",["$","$L10",null,{"term":"A2C"}]," is generally preferred in practice because synchronous updates produce more stable\nlearning — the additional variance from asynchronous gradient staleness typically\noutweighs the decorrelation benefit."]}]
